carWaleTake.verdict: Designed to plug the gap between the Baleno and Brezza, the new Fronx has quite a lot going for it. Namely the stance, a long list of features, a mature ride and a refined set of capable motors. Yet another winner from Maruti!
carWaleTake.pros:
• The Fronx’s coupe SUV styling gives it a distinct and noteworthy stance.
  • Higher variants are well-equipped with all the essential comfort and convenience features.
  • The suspension setup offers good shock absorption, resulting in a comfortable ride quality.
  • Both engine options deliver adequate performance, making city and highway driving easy.
carWaleTake.cons:
• Unlike most SUVs, its seating position isn’t as high, limiting the commanding view of the road.
  • The 1.0-litre turbo-petrol engine feels a bit sluggish below 2,500rpm.
  • The rear headroom is limited, which can be uncomfortable for taller passengers.
carWaleTake.reviewShortDescription: The Fronx is part of Maruti’s SUV and crossover lineup and it sits below the Brezza and the Jimny. Naturally it’s got all the butch design ingredients to make it look more substantial than the Baleno. Let’s take a deep dive into the positioning of the new Fronx.

reviewDescription.content:
It may look like a small, funky crossover but the new Maruti Suzuki Fronx is actually based on the Baleno, which isn’t a bad thing at all because the new Baleno is a pretty impressive car. And after having driven it, it’s safe to conclude that the Fronx has all the makings to become yet another strong seller for Maruti. Now, let’s take a deep dive into the positioning of the new Fronx.  

The Fronx is part of Maruti’s SUV and crossover lineup and it sits below the Brezza and the Jimny. Naturally it’s got all the butch design ingredients to make it look more substantial than the Baleno. So you get a large bumper with integrated headlights, a grille that’s more in your face and a pretty substantial dose of black cladding all around. I like how the Fronx looks overall but the rear is undoubtedly my favorite angle of the car. It’s a genuinely good looking car and Maruti has done a good job of differentiating it from the Baleno. 

I especially like the detailing on the taillights and the one piece light bar that joins the taillight units. In terms of size and road presence, the Fronx is 3,995 millimeters in length, 1,765 millimeters in width and its 1,550 millimeters off the ground. Now when you compare these with the Baleno you will see that the Fronx is a tad bit more substantial although the real world difference in size will be not be perceptible. Also, the wheelbase on the Fronx and the Baleno is identical so it’s not like you are going to get more room inside the cabin.

reviewDescription.pageName: Is the cabin of the Fronx any good?
reviewDescription.content:
To put it short, it’s exactly like the Baleno. Everything from the design, the layout and seat upholstery is familiar affair. That said, the cabin’s design itself is unlike Maruti’s other hatchbacks and is, in fact, way more upmarket. In terms of familiarity, everything from the steering wheel, the dials, the center console and the seats are from the Baleno. Now I do appreciate the premium ambience of this cabin but it looks identical to other similarly priced Maruti cars. The basic design, the colors and textures used in the Brezza and the Grand Vitara are pretty much the same so a little originality here would have been nice, especially since so much work has gone into the exterior design of this car. 

Given that it’s based on the Baleno, space and comfort levels remain unchanged for the Fronx. Jump in the back and you will notice how accommodating the rear is and spacious it feels despite the dark interior theme. There is a good deal of legroom and the rear seat is supportive as well. It might be a bit too flat for some people but good under thigh support and cushioning more than make up for it. The Fronx sits higher than the Baleno but it’s because of the mechanical changes and the cabin height is the same as the Baleno. Given its crossover positioning, I would have liked a higher driving position and more headroom at the rear.   

So there are five variants including Sigma, Delta, Delta+, Zeta, and Alpha. As for the features, it doesn’t matter what variant you choose, you will get dual airbags, ESP, hill hold assist, reverse parking sensors, rear defogger, keyless entry, dual-tone interior, 60:40 split for the rear seat, front and rear power windows, automatic climate control and a flat bottom steering wheel as standard. Go for the most expensive Alpha variant and you will get all the new age features. 

There’s a heads up display with turn-by-turn navigation, a 360 view camera, wireless charger, cruise control, 16-inch dual-tone alloy wheels and a feature loaded infotainment system with a 9-inch screen, wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto. You also get side and curtain airbags, tilt and telescopic adjustment for the steering wheel, height adjustment for the driver’s seat, rear AC vents, push button start, leather-wrapped steering wheel and LED headlights as standard on this top-spec variant. Oddly enough, a rear armrest is missing in the entire line-up.

reviewDescription.pageName: Is the Fronx good to drive?
reviewDescription.content:
The Fronx comes with two engine and three gearbox options. There’s the familiar 1.2-litre four-cylinder naturally aspirated engine which can be had with either a 5-speed manual or a 5-speed AMT gearbox. What you see here is the 1-litre, three-cylinder turbocharged engine which has made a comeback with the Fronx. To be sold with either a 5-speed manual or a 6-speed torque converter automatic, we have seen this engine in the older gen Baleno RS although this time around, it is locally made. Unfortunately, the 1-litre turbo engine can be had in the top three variants only whereas the 1.2 engine can be had with the base and mid-level variants.   

The 1-litre Fronx makes 99bhp and 150Nm of torque which is spread across 2,000-4,500rpm, meaning you will have to stretch its legs a bit for maximum punch. I remember driving the Baleno RS with this engine at the Buddh International Circuit over half a decade ago and coming back with a reassuring grin. It’s a gutsy little engine that loves to rev – naturally there is some turbo lag below 2,500rpm but once you get it working in the midrange it will not hesitate one bit and pull you forward with enough gusto. 

Now because it’s a relatively small turbo, the mid-range surge is smooth and when it’s on boost, the buildup in speed is linear making the Fronx quite user friendly. It also responds well to throttle inputs, winding out with a slightly throaty induction bark. The Fronx’s ground clearance is 20mm higher than the Baleno’s. Maruti tells us it’s because of the larger profile tires and different spring rates. 

As for the suspension setup, it feels similar to the Baleno but because there’s a tad bit more travel, it is able to cope with deep potholes and speed humps in a slightly better manner, in the sense that it doesn’t knock as much, especially when the suspension decompresses. The ride quality on the Fronx is another big plus point – it’s comfortable, refined and when it comes to ironing out irregularities over poorly built roads, it’s got that layer of maturity that we have seen in Maruti’s current crossover line-up.

The new Maruti Suzuki Fronx is a brilliant little crossover. It’s got the looks, a long features list, commendable ride quality and an impressive turbo petrol engine. It’s biggest plus point though will be its positioning in Maruti’s line-up. To be sold between the Baleno and the Brezza, the Fronx has created its own niche in a market which has young buyers who want something different, something with character. 

Bookings for the Fronx are already open but we do not know what the pricing is going to be like. Now we expect the 1.2-litre variants to be priced around a lakh or so more than the equivalent Baleno variant but there will be a significant premium that Maruti is likely to charge for the 1-litre Fronx, especially with the torque converter automatic gearbox because it is an expensive drivetrain to produce, after all.

Pictures by Kapil Angane

userReviewsWithImages.Title: My Car Buying Journey – Why I Chose the Maruti Fronx
userReviewsWithImages.Description:
I wanted to share my experience with buying a car, it might help someone who is in the same dilemma I was in. 
I started my search by looking at used cars, I checked out quite a few, but he prices were surprisingly high, and most of the sellers weren't even open to negotiation. On top of that, I found that loan interest for used cars is significantly higher than that for new cars. so decided to for a brand-new vehicle.

My initial shortlist included Honda Amaze, Baleno, and Fronx.  Honda didn't offer any discounts, while maruti had some attractive offers. Between the Baleno and the front, my family preferred the front Delta Plus variant. I was also stuck in the same confusion many buyers face: should I go for the top-end Baleno or the mid model for almost the same price?  Let me be clear - go with fronx, you won't be disappointed. Yes, the Baleno top variant offers more features, but the front stands out in looks and road presence. There are some missing features in the base and mid variants of fronx, like seat height adjustment, push button start, reverse cam, and armrest. When someone is spending more than 1 million, it's reasonable to expect at least some of these features. Another thing to keep in mind is AMT lag; it's noticeable, especially while overtaking, you need to plan your moves carefully, as it doesn't deliver that much power. Also, the interiors are a bit dark, I felt maruti could have gone with lighter shades to enhance the cabin feel. That said, the car is spacious, and the engines feel very refined. I have been getting a mileage of around 12-14 in the city and 15-19 on highways (I haven't completed the first service yet)

1. The design and styling are unmatched at this price range.
2. Good road presence.
3. Availability of service centers.
4. And the resale value and fuel efficiency make it a sensible choice.

To conclude, as ordinary salaried people, we all have limitations when it comes to spending. There is no such thing as a perfect car; choose one that fits your needs and budget. Don't pour all your savings into it or take unnecessary loan burdens.
Pick what's affordable, stay happy, and enjoy the drive.

userReviewsWithImages.Title: Fronx is Rock
userReviewsWithImages.Description: Overall, the Fronx CNG is a good option for those looking for a fuel-efficient, comfortable, and stylish compact SUV with a strong resale value, especially if prioritizing city driving and budget-friendly ownership.

userReviewsWithImages.Title: Fronx is a good budget-friendly car
userReviewsWithImages.Description:
In this budget, Fronx is a good budget-friendly car in the subcompact crossover SUV segment. I have ridden my car  40000 km, and the riding comfort and the suspension setup are very good and provide a balance between comfort and handling. It is good for city driving conditions. I love the design like a sports coupe rear design and the front like a futuristic premium design. 
1.2L K12N Dualjet 14 petrol engine (89 hp) with a 5-speed manual transmission provides better performance to this car. The total service cost I had for 40000km in 8 months is ₹ 8400.    Pros: *I like the design *Comfortable ride *Fuel efficiency* Value for Money. 
Cons: *The coupe-style rear design compromises rear visibility for drivers who are of a lower height. *Rear seat Headspace is lower if a person who has a height above 170cm may his head have a space between 4 fingers.

userReviewsWithImages.Title: Maruti Suzuki Fronx Alpha
userReviewsWithImages.Description:
The Maruti Suzuki Baleno is like the most common sense car today. Maruti Suzuki Fronx, which basically is the same car, had some big shoes to fill. From the outside, the Maruti Suzuki Fronx definitely does look great. A lot of people confuse it with the bigger Maruti Suzuki Grand Vitara, which also feels good. However, the moment you sit inside, you realise it is the same familiar yet reliable Maruti. It's simple, plasticky and cheap, but you know it will last. Since a 360 camera was a requirement for me, I went straight for the top-end turbo automatic. It's been 8 months and already done 15000+ kms. This includes a lot of drives around Bangalore and a few drives from Bangalore to Goa and Kolhapur. The suspension has been slightly stiffened, and the car is slightly raised compared to the Baleno. This gives the car good driving behaviour, and I can definitely vouch for it. You sit a bit lower than in a comparable SUV, and the seat cushions are a bit soft, but the driver gets all the adjustments, so you can sit comfortably. When you start the car, there is a weird vibration in the 3-cylinder engine. It's a little noisy and the car shakes in a start. This can sometimes catch you off guard, especially if you have only driven 4-cylinder engines. However, once you start moving, the car just comes alive. 
On regular drives in the city, you will probably feel a bit of the suspension, but it is definitely a lot of fun to drive. 
On one such day, I was travelling from Hubli to Goa via the Anmod ghat and non-existent roads. In front of me was a Skoda Slavia, carefully manoeuvring through the potholes. As I was going through the broken roads, I started getting more and more confident in the ability of my car. At the end of it, I could gun the car passed the Skoda and I didn't feel a thing. Fronx is truly an amazing car. Maybe just the Kia Sonet is better for a little more money since it feels a lot more premium inside. If you love to drive, the punchy turbo engine and the ability to confidently drive on any road make it a truly great car.

summary:
When was the Maruti Fronx launched?

The Maruti Suzuki Fronx was launched in India on 24 April, 2023.

What variants does it get?

The Baleno-based Fronx is available in five variants – Sigma, Delta, Delta+, Zeta, and Alpha.

What features are available in the Maruti Fronx?

The Fronx features LED headlamps, LED taillights, 16-inch dual-tone alloy wheels, contrast-coloured faux skid plates, and silver roof-rails. This crossover has a seating capacity of five occupants and boasts of equipment such as a 360-degree camera, HUD, and a nine-inch SmartPlay Pro+ touchscreen infotainment system and supports Apple CarPlay and Android Auto connectivity. It even gets six airbags, cruise control, UV-cut glass, rear AC vents, and a wireless charger.

What are the engine, performance, and specifications of the Maruti Fronx?

The Maruti Fronx is powered either by a 1.2-litre four-cylinder NA petrol engine or a 1.0-litre three-cylinder turbo-petrol engine. The former produces an output of 89bhp and 113Nm of torque, while the turbo-petrol churns out 99bhp and 147Nm of torque. Gearbox options include a five-speed manual, AMT, and a six-speed torque converter automatic unit.

Is the Maruti Fronx a safe car?

The Fronx coupe-SUV has not been tested by any NCAP body yet.

What are the rivals to the Maruti Fronx?

The Fronx competes against the Hyundai Venue, Kia Sonet, Renault Kiger, Nissan Magnite, Mahindra XUV300, and the Tata Nexon.

Last updated on 10 July, 2024.

metaTags.seoText: The price of Maruti Fronx, a 5 seater Sub-Compact SUV, ranges from Rs. 6.85  - 11.98 Lakh. It is available in 14 variants, with engine options ranging from 998 to 1197 cc and a choice of 2 transmissions: Manual and Automatic. Fronx has an NCAP rating of 1 stars and comes with 6 airbags. Maruti Fronx has a ground clearance (unladen) of 190 mm  and is available in 10 colours. Users have reported a mileage of 20.01 to 22.89 kmpl for Fronx.

faqs.question: What is the avg ex-showroom price of Maruti Suzuki Fronx base model?
faqs.answer: The avg ex-showroom price of Maruti Suzuki Fronx base model is Rs. 6.85 Lakh which includes a registration cost of Rs. 81688, insurance premium of Rs. 33313 and additional charges of Rs. 850.

faqs.question: What is the avg ex-showroom price of Maruti Suzuki Fronx top model?
faqs.answer: The avg ex-showroom price of Maruti Suzuki Fronx top model is Rs. 11.98 Lakh which includes a registration cost of Rs. 152861, insurance premium of Rs. 45566 and additional charges of Rs. 850.

faqs.question: What is the real world versus claimed mileage of Maruti Suzuki Fronx?
faqs.answer:
The company claimed mileage of Maruti Suzuki Fronx is 22.89 kmpl, while when CarWale experts tested it, they found the mileage to be 14.25 kmpl in city 
                    and 19.09 kmpl on highways. As per users, the mileage came to be 19 to 27 kmpl 
                in the real world.

faqs.question: What is the seating capacity in Maruti Suzuki Fronx?
faqs.answer: Maruti Suzuki Fronx is a 5 seater car.

faqs.question: What are the dimensions of Maruti Suzuki Fronx?
faqs.answer: The dimensions of Maruti Suzuki Fronx include its length of 3995 mm, width of 1765 mm and height of 1550 mm. The wheelbase of the Maruti Suzuki Fronx is 2520 mm.

faqs.question: Does Maruti Suzuki Fronx get a sunroof?
faqs.answer: No, Maruti Suzuki Fronx doesn't have a sunroof.

faqs.question: Does Maruti Suzuki Fronx have cruise control?
faqs.answer: Yes, 4 out of 14 variants of Maruti Suzuki Fronx have cruise control function. With the Cruise control enabled you can take your foot off the accelerator and move at a fixed speed constantly provided the road system permits this.

faqs.question: How many airbags does Maruti Suzuki Fronx get?
faqs.answer: The top Model of Maruti Suzuki Fronx has 6 airbags. The Fronx has driver,  front passenger,  2 curtain,  driver side and  front passenger side airbags.

faqs.question: Does Maruti Suzuki Fronx get ABS?
faqs.answer: Yes, all variants of Maruti Suzuki Fronx have ABS. ABS is a great accident prevention technology, allowing drivers to steer while braking hard.

engineContent.title: Maruti Suzuki Fronx Engine and Performance

pageList.content:
Positives:

  • The 1.0-litre three-cylinder Turbo Boosterjet petrol engine produces 99bhp of power and 147.6Nm of torque. It’s mated to a five-speed manual or six-speed automatic transmission (torque convertor with paddle shifters).
  • The ARAI-rated mileage is 21.5kmpl, but CarWale tests show 14.25kmpl in cities and 19.09kmpl on the highways. This can take you from 527km to 706km on a full tank, thanks to its 37-litre fuel capacity.
  • The light feel of the clutch pedal and short gear lever deliver a comfortable gear shifting experience even while shifting quickly.
  • The engine sounds throaty while accelerating and can go from 0 to 60 in just 5.3 seconds (claimed).
  • The mid-range power delivery is alert punchy, partly due to its hybrid system.
  • The ride quality of the Maruti Fronx is comfortable due to the suspension setup's ability to absorb bumps.
  • The 190mm ground clearance is also a positive considering the Indian road conditions.
  • The 1.0-litre turbo petrol engine is available in Delta Plus, Zeta and Alpha trims, with the 6-speed AMT is only available in Zeta and Alpha trims.

Negatives:

  • While driving below 2,500rpm, the engine response from the 1.0-litre turbo is slightly lacking.

Maruti Suzuki Fronx Key Specifications:

• The 1.2-litre K-Series Dual Jet, Dual VVT engine makes 88bhp and 113Nm of torque and gets a five-speed manual or five-speed automatic gearbox. 
  • The Idle Stat Stop technology (ISS) on this engine spec helps keep the fuel consumption in check, especially in the slow moving city traffic.
  • The ARAI-reported mileage of 1.2-litre Maruti Fronx is 21.79kmpl for the manual while the AMT boasts a mileage of up to 22.89kmpl. 
  • The suspension setup and 190mm ground clearance (20mm higher than Maruti Baleno it’s based on) helps it coast through Indian roads with a considerable comfort. 
  • The 1.2-litre petrol engine with five-speed MT is available in Sigma, Delta, Delta Plus and Delta Plus (O) trims, while the Delta, Delta Plus and Delta Plus (O) get the 5-speed AMT option as well.
Key Specifications:

• The 1.2-litre Dual Jet, Dual VVT engine in the Maruti Suzuki Fronx gets an additional CNG option, making 76bhp and 98.5Nm of torque. Meanwhile, the power figures in the petrol mode remain the same as the petrol-only version. 
  • This engine option is mated to a five-speed manual transmission and gets a claimed fuel efficiency of 28.51km/kg.
  • The combination of a 55-litre (water equivalent) CNG storage tank along with the default 37-litre petrol tank give ample range to the Maruti Fronx.
  • The CNG option is only available in Sigma and Delta trims. 
Key Specifications:

featureContent.title: Maruti Suzuki Fronx Convenience and Technology

pageList.content:
Positives:

  • The Maruti Fronx has a well-designed cabin with leather-wrapped flat-bottom steering, premium-feeling upholstery, steering mounted controls, and dual-tone interiors.
  • It also features, an eight-way seat adjustment including height (for the driver seat), cruise control, paddle-shifters, push-button start, keyless entry, and hill hold assist to ease the driving experience.
  • It features a decent 360-degree camera system with guide lines to help with driver awareness.
  • The nine-inch Arkamys SmartPlay Pro+ HD infotainment system gets wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto. The presence of wireless charger, 40+ connected car features, and heads up display (HUD) with turn-by-turn navigation make it decently techy. 
  • For the passengers, the rear seats feel spacious and airy despite the dual-tone interiors with good under-thigh support. 
  • The rear AC vents and fast-charging USB, and the overall sound system with 4 speakers and 2 tweeters enhance the experience.

Negatives:

  • The lack of shoulder room sticks out when three passengers are seated in the rear seats.
  • As Maruti Fronx is positioned as a crossover or compact SUV (as Maruti calls it), its cabin height should have been more than the Baleno’s (for extra headroom).

Positives:

  • The Maruti Suzuki Fronx gets six airbags (two front, two side and two curtain), Electronic Stability Control (ESP) with hill-hold assist and rollover mitigation, ABS, and traction control.
  • In addition, it gets a 360-degree camera, reverse parking sensors and rear defogger for improved positional awareness for the driver. 
  • For further driver and passenger protection, it gets ISOFIX points for child safety and 3-point (Emergency Locking Retractor) ELR seat belts that allow easy retractability in normal conditions along with locking during emergencies. 

Negatives:

  • The Maruti Fronx is yet to be tested by the Global or Bharat NCAP authorities for crash safety ratings. The Maruti Baleno (which the Fronx is based on) did get 4 star Bharat NCAP ratings. 
  • Advanced Driver-Assistance Systems (ADAS) functionality could’ve added a major upgrade to its safety and driving assistance feature list.

interiorContent.title: Maruti Suzuki Fronx Interior and Exterior Styling

pageList.content:
Positives:

  • The Maruti Fronx has sharp aerodynamic design, bold front and rear fascia, and 16-inch dual-tone alloy wheels to enhance its visual appeal.
  • While based on the Baleno, the higher suspension setup and larger tyre profile gives it a more commanding stance, well complemented by its muscular design elements. 
  • The LED headlights, LED DRLs, and fully-connected LED taillights give it a sleek and modern look. 
  • On the inside, the Fronx gets a plush dual-tone upholstery, flat-bottom steering wheel, 9-inch HD infotainment system, and dual-tone dashboard with metal accents.
  • While the overall layout and seat upholstery is similar to the Baleno, the cabin’s design itself is refreshing when compared to other Maruti hatchbacks and feels more upmarket.
  • It comes in a total of ten different colour options including three in dual tone. Namely: NEXA Blue (Celestial), Arctic White, Splendid Silver, Grandeur Grey, Earthen Brown, Opulent Red, Bluish Black, Earthen Brown with Bluish Black Roof, Splendid Silver with Bluish Back Roof and Opulent Red + Black. 

Negatives:

  • The basic design, texture and colours are comparable with similarly priced Maruti cars, a bit more uniqueness would be welcome.

page.content: The Maruti Suzuki Fronx has an impressive mix of performance, looks and features. The 1.0-litre turbo engine is enjoyable to drive and offers a 21.5kmpl mileage. As for the comfort and features, the dual-tone interiors, 9-inch HD touchscreen, and 360-degree camera make it a versatile option in the segment.
